task_completion.description: Verify the agent researched the route on a mapping service.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window open to a mapping service (e.g., Google Maps, Bing Maps, MapQuest) showing a route from San Francisco, CA to Los Angeles, CA.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent checked the weather for Los Angeles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window open to a weather service (e.g., weather.com, weather.gov, AccuWeather) displaying the current weather or forecast for Los Angeles, CA.

task_completion.description: Verify the itinerary file was created at the correct location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with the file named 'pacific_coast_itinerary.txt' or the file icon visible on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/).

task_completion.description: Verify the document includes the client's name and a route summary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text inside 'pacific_coast_itinerary.txt' explicitly contains the name 'Margaret Chen' and a summary or title indicating a Pacific Coast Highway road trip from San Francisco to Los Angeles.

task_completion.description: Verify the document includes the driving distance and estimated time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text inside the itinerary file states the total driving distance (approximately 460-500 miles) and the estimated driving time (approximately 9-11 hours).

task_completion.description: Verify the document includes at least three notable waypoints with descriptions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text inside the itinerary file lists at least three cities or landmarks along the route (e.g., Monterey, Big Sur, Santa Barbara, Malibu) accompanied by brief descriptions for sightseeing.

task_completion.description: Verify the document includes the Los Angeles weather outlook and practical travel tips.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text inside the itinerary file contains a section describing the weather conditions in Los Angeles and a section providing practical travel tips for the road trip.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a graphical web browser to gather information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) being used to access map and weather websites, rather than relying solely on hallucinated knowledge or terminal curl commands.

integrity.description: Verify the file was actually saved to the Desktop.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor should show the file is saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/pacific_coast_itinerary.txt' (e.g., no 'unsaved' asterisk in the title bar), or the file can be seen created on the desktop background or in a file manager.

integrity.description: Verify the weather data appears to be sourced from the actual search rather than a generic placeholder.
integrity.visual_evidence: The weather outlook in the text file should contain specific details (like temperature, conditions) that align with a real forecast, not generic template text like '[Insert Weather Here]'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Pacific Coast Highway (Highway 1) route from San Francisco to Los Angeles is approximately 460-500 miles and takes about 9-11 hours of continuous driving. The client's name must be exactly 'Margaret Chen'. The file must be saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/pacific_coast_itinerary.txt'. Valid waypoints should be authentic coastal stops (e.g., Monterey, Big Sur, Morro Bay, Santa Barbara, Malibu). Inland US-101 stops like Santa Maria, Solvang, and Paso Robles are not on the PCH proper.
